CREATE TABLE test_table (id Int32, a Int32, b Int32);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE a <= b;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE a <= b AND a > 0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE a <= b;
SELECT id, a, b FROM test_table WHERE a <= 10;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE a <= (SELECT MAX(b) FROM test_table);
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE a <= (SELECT MIN(a) FROM test_table);
CREATE TABLE secondary_table (id Int32, a Int32, b Int32);
INSERT INTO secondary_table VALUES (1, 2, 5), (2, 8, 8), (3, 10, 5);
SELECT st.id FROM secondary_table st JOIN test_table tt ON st.a <= tt.b;
SELECT tt.id FROM test_table tt WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM secondary_table st WHERE tt.a <= st.b);
SELECT tt.id FROM test_table tt LEFT JOIN secondary_table st ON tt.a <= st.b WHERE st.id IS NULL;
WITH (SELECT MAX(a) FROM test_table) AS max_a SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE a <= max_a;
SELECT id, a, b FROM test_table ORDER BY a, b ASC;
CREATE TABLE test_numeric_table (id Int32, a Decimal(10, 2), b Decimal(10, 2));
INSERT INTO test_numeric_table VALUES (1, 1.50, 2.20), (2, 3.00, 3.00), (3, 5.10, 5.00);
SELECT * FROM test_numeric_table WHERE a <= b;
SELECT AVG(b) FROM test_numeric_table WHERE a <= b;
SELECT tt.id FROM test_table tt CROSS JOIN secondary_table st WHERE tt.a <= st.b;
